features.name: Agent Teams
features.one_liner: Multiple agents collaborating on a shared task, each with different roles and specializations.

features.name: Authentication & OAuth
features.one_liner: Flexible authentication with OAuth, API keys, dynamic key refresh, and keychain integration.
features.quick_start: claude (OAuth runs automatically on first launch)

features.name: Auto Mode
features.one_liner: Claude automatically decides whether to think through a problem or jump straight into action.

detection.tip: Auto mode uses a background classifier to approve safe actions automatically, so you don't have to click allow on every tool use.

features.name: Auto-Memory
features.one_liner: Claude automatically remembers your preferences and project details across sessions.

features.name: Background Agents
features.one_liner: Kick off long-running tasks that keep working even after you close your terminal.

detection.tip: Claude has been working for {tool_count}+ tool uses on this task. You can press Ctrl+B to background it and keep working on something else.

features.name: Bare Mode
features.one_liner: Stripped-down execution mode that skips hooks, skills, plugins, MCP servers, auto memory, and CLAUDE.md for maximum speed.

features.name: Bash Tool & Background Tasks
features.one_liner: Execute shell commands with permission controls and run long-lived processes in the background.
features.quick_start: Claude uses this automatically for shell commands

features.name: Bedrock Integration
features.one_liner: Run Claude Code through AWS Bedrock using your organization's AWS credentials and billing.

features.name: Channels (Mobile & Slack)
features.one_liner: Access Claude Code from mobile devices, Slack, and other messaging platforms via channel servers.

features.name: Claude Desktop App
features.one_liner: The Code tab in the Claude Desktop app for Mac and Windows — full GUI with visual diff review, live app preview, computer use, and parallel sessions.

features.name: Claude in Chrome
features.one_liner: Browser extension that lets Claude control and interact with web pages — navigate, click, fill forms, extract data.

features.name: Computer Use
features.one_liner: macOS screen control — Claude can open apps, click, type, scroll, and take screenshots to interact with native GUI applications.
features.quick_start: Enable via /mcp menu (macOS only, Pro/Max plan)

features.name: Context Management
features.one_liner: Monitor and optimize your context window usage with auto-compaction and usage insights.

detection.tip: This session has been going for a while ({tool_count}+ tool uses). Try /context to check your context usage and /compact to free up space.

features.name: Copy Picker
features.one_liner: Select and copy specific code blocks from Claude's responses without manual highlighting.

detection.tip: Use /copy to pick and copy specific code blocks from Claude's responses. Press 'w' to write to a file instead.

features.name: Cost & Stats Tracking
features.one_liner: Monitor token usage and API costs with /cost during your session.

detection.tip: Run /cost to see your current session costs and token usage.

features.name: Custom Agent Definitions
features.one_liner: Define your own specialized agents with specific models, tools, system prompts, and constraints.

features.name: Deep Links
features.one_liner: Launch Claude Code sessions from external apps and docs using claude-cli:// URIs.

detection.tip: Claude Code supports deep links with the claude-cli:// protocol. You can link directly to sessions from docs or scripts.

features.name: Doctor & Debug
features.one_liner: Diagnose setup issues with `claude doctor` and enable verbose logging with `--verbose`.

features.name: Extended Thinking
features.one_liner: Claude thinks deeper before responding, showing its reasoning process in real-time.

detection.tip: Claude has been doing heavy exploration ({reads} reads, {searches} searches) without producing output. Try toggling extended thinking with Option+T for deeper reasoning.

features.name: GitHub Integration
features.one_liner: Full GitHub workflow support — PRs, issues, reviews, and CI, all from your terminal.
features.quick_start: Ask Claude to create a PR

detection.tip: You ran git commit and git push manually. Claude can handle the full PR workflow — just ask it to create a PR.

features.name: Grep & Glob Tools
features.one_liner: Fast codebase search — Grep searches file contents with regex, Glob finds files by name patterns.
features.quick_start: Claude uses these automatically for code search

features.name: Hooks System
features.one_liner: Event-driven automation that triggers shell commands, HTTP calls, or AI prompts on Claude Code events.

detection.tip: Hooks run shell commands or AI prompts in response to Claude Code events. Run /hooks to see what's configured.

features.name: Image Support
features.one_liner: Drag-and-drop or paste images into Claude Code — screenshots, diagrams, mockups, all understood.
features.quick_start: Drag an image into the terminal or Ctrl+V to paste

detection.tip: You can share images with Claude — drag a file into the terminal, paste with Ctrl+V, or provide a file path.

features.name: JetBrains Extension
features.one_liner: Claude Code integrated into IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm, and all other JetBrains IDEs.

features.name: Keybindings
features.one_liner: Customize keyboard shortcuts and add chord bindings for full control over your input experience.

detection.tip: Run /keybindings to customize keyboard shortcuts. You can rebind submit, cancel, voice, and add chord bindings.

features.name: LSP Integration
features.one_liner: Language Server Protocol support for go-to-definition, find-references, hover docs, and smarter code navigation.

features.name: Loop & Cron Scheduling
features.one_liner: Run prompts or commands on recurring intervals — set it and forget it.

detection.tip: You've run `{command}` {count} times without changes in between. Loop can automate this — try `/loop 5m {command}`.

features.name: MCP Servers
features.one_liner: Connect Claude to external tools and data sources via the Model Context Protocol.
features.quick_start: claude mcp add my-server -- npx server-package

detection.tip: MCP servers connect Claude to external tools and data sources. Try: claude mcp add my-server -- npx server-package

features.name: Managed Settings (Enterprise)
features.one_liner: Organization-wide policy configuration for controlling permissions, tools, models, and behaviors across all users.

features.name: Model Selection & Effort Control
features.one_liner: Switch between Claude models and dial in effort levels to balance depth vs speed.

detection.tip: You can switch models on the fly with /model — choose between Opus, Sonnet, and Haiku for different speed/quality tradeoffs.

features.name: Permission System & Sandbox
features.one_liner: Control what Claude can and can't do with granular permissions, approval workflows, and sandbox isolation.

features.name: Plan Mode
features.one_liner: Claude explores your codebase and builds a strategic implementation plan before writing a single line of code.
features.quick_start: Shift+Tab to cycle to plan mode

features.name: Plugin System
features.one_liner: Install community plugins that add skills, hooks, MCP servers, agents, and more.

detection.tip: Browse and install community plugins with /plugin. Plugins add skills, hooks, MCP servers, and more.

features.name: Prompt Suggestions
features.one_liner: Auto-complete suggestions for commands, file paths, and common prompts as you type.
features.quick_start: Just start typing — suggestions appear automatically

features.name: Read/Write/Edit Tools
features.one_liner: Core file manipulation — read files with line numbers, write new files, and make surgical edits with exact string replacement.
features.quick_start: Claude uses these automatically for file operations

features.name: Remote Control Sessions
features.one_liner: Continue local Claude Code sessions from any device via claude.ai/code or the Claude mobile app, with your full local environment still running.
features.quick_start: Run `claude remote-control` or use `/remote-control` inside a session to make it available at claude.ai/code

detection.tip: Use /remote-control to access your session from a phone or browser. Share the URL with teammates for pair programming.

features.name: Rules (CLAUDE.md)
features.one_liner: Customize Claude's behavior with markdown instruction files loaded into every session.

detection.tip: This project doesn't have a CLAUDE.md yet. Run /init to create one — it sets persistent instructions so Claude follows your team's conventions in every session.

features.name: Session Management
features.one_liner: Name, resume, fork, and rewind sessions so you never lose your place.

detection.tip: You can pick up exactly where you left off with `claude --resume`. It restores the full conversation.

features.name: Skills System
features.one_liner: Create custom slash commands with markdown files for reusable, shareable workflows.

detection.tip: You can create custom slash commands by adding SKILL.md files in .claude/skills/. Each skill gets its own /command.

features.name: Subagents
features.one_liner: Spawn specialized child agents that handle subtasks independently with their own context windows.
features.quick_start: Claude spawns these automatically for complex tasks

detection.tip: Claude just edited {files} different files ({file_list}). For large-scale changes like this, subagents can parallelize the work across multiple agents.

features.name: Task System
features.one_liner: Create, track, and manage tasks with dependencies so you can see exactly where complex work stands.

features.name: Terminal Setup
features.one_liner: Optimized configurations for popular terminals so Claude Code looks and works its best.

detection.tip: Run /terminal-setup to configure your terminal for the best Claude Code experience — fixes Shift+Enter, key handling, and rendering.

features.name: Theme & UI Customization
features.one_liner: Customize syntax highlighting, prompt bar color, and spinner tips to make Claude Code yours.

detection.tip: Run /theme to change syntax highlighting. Use /color to set prompt bar color. Customize spinner tips in settings.

features.name: Transcript Mode
features.one_liner: Scroll through and search your full conversation history with Claude.

detection.tip: Press Ctrl+O to toggle transcript mode — it shows detailed tool usage and expanded results from Claude's work.

features.name: VS Code Extension
features.one_liner: Full Claude Code experience inside VS Code with inline chat, session tabs, and sidebar management.
features.quick_start: Install 'Claude Code' from VS Code marketplace

features.name: Vertex AI Integration
features.one_liner: Run Claude Code through Google Cloud Vertex AI with your GCP project billing and credentials.

features.name: Vim Mode
features.one_liner: Full vim motions and text objects in the Claude Code input editor.

detection.tip: Claude Code has built-in vim mode with full motions and text objects. Try /vim to enable it.

features.name: Voice Mode
features.one_liner: Talk to Claude Code with your voice -- hold a key, speak, release to send.

detection.tip: You can speak to Claude instead of typing. Try /voice — hold Space to talk, release to send.

features.name: WebFetch Tool
features.one_liner: Fetch and analyze web content — documentation, API responses, GitHub issues, and more.
features.quick_start: Ask Claude to fetch any URL

detection.tip: Claude can fetch and analyze any web page. Just ask it to read a URL — docs, API responses, GitHub issues.

features.name: Worktrees
features.one_liner: Run agents in isolated git worktrees so they never block your main branch.

detection.tip: Claude just edited {files} different files ({file_list}). Worktrees let agents work in isolated git branches so changes don't interfere with your main work.
